user_prompt: also, for the button types that are not GPIO, as in, cannot have an ISR, I still want the poll function to record the down time and such
agent_reply: Updated [Buttons.cpp](c:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/lib/Buttons/Buttons.cpp) and [Buttons.h](c:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/lib/Buttons/Buttons.h) to behave more like the old reference code.
Key changes:
- Added old-style debounce using `BTN_DEBOUNCE`, with a local default if it is not defined.
- Added `clr_time`, volatile press/down counters, and clear-time behavior like the reference.
- GPIO buttons now attach an object-aware ISR via `attachInterruptArg`, so the class-owned `press_cnt` is updated by the interrupt.
- GPIO `poll()` now clears `down_time` on release and can optionally catch missed ISR edges under `TRY_CATCH_MISSED_GPIO_ISR`.
- Touch and power buttons still use `Button::poll()`, so their `down_time`, held time, and press counters are recorded even though they cannot use ISR.
- App handlers for ISR-backed GPIO buttons are dispatched later from `poll()`, not inside the ISR.
I still couldn’t run a PlatformIO build because `pio`/`platformio` isn’t available in this shell.

Key changes:
- Added old-style debounce using `BTN_DEBOUNCE`, with a local default if it is not defined.
- Added `clr_time`, volatile press/down counters, and clear-time behavior like the reference.
- GPIO buttons now attach an object-aware ISR via `attachInterruptArg`, so the class-owned `press_cnt` is updated by the interrupt.
- GPIO `poll()` now clears `down_time` on release and can optionally catch missed ISR edges under `TRY_CATCH_MISSED_GPIO_ISR`.
- Touch and power buttons still use `Button::poll()`, so their `down_time`, held time, and press counters are recorded even though they cannot use ISR.
- App handlers for ISR-backed GPIO buttons are dispatched later from `poll()`, not inside the ISR.

Implemented `Hotel` as a namespace-backed power manager in [Hotel.h](C:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/lib/Hotel/Hotel.h) and [Hotel.cpp](C:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/lib/Hotel/Hotel.cpp).
What’s included:
- State machine from the README: blocked, active thresholds, light sleep states, shutdown.
- `pollCore0()` / `pollCore1()` synchronization before light sleep.
- User activity API, including ISR-safe activity notification.
- Blocking while recording, Bluetooth is active, Wi-Fi is active, or audio mode is active.
- Transition-only CPU/backlight changes with debug transition logging.
- `esp_timer_get_time()` wrapper for sleep-safe elapsed time.
- M5 PMIC shutdown, M5 light sleep when ready, timer-only ESP light sleep fallback before `M5.begin()`.
I also wired it into:
- [main.cpp](C:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/src/main.cpp): both core loops call Hotel polling.
- [Buttons.cpp](C:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/lib/Buttons/Buttons.cpp): button activity resets Hotel timer.
- [FlyGui.cpp](C:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FlyGui/FlyGui.cpp): touch activity resets Hotel timer.

Verification: `pio run` still fails before compiling `Hotel` because of the existing `SdCard` namespace collision with SdFat in `src/mic_sd_recorder_demo.cpp`. The error is the same prior blocker: repo `namespace SdCard` conflicts with SdFat’s global `SdCard` typedef.
